title: Prototype: The Soda Comet Incident
summary: A fizzy comet made of weaponized soda ice drifts toward the Starship Shamples. Chat must decide whether to study it, fight it, or let Pedro the Raccoon solve carbonation with electricity, which is the wrong kind of confidence but still technically confidence.

nodes.title: A Carbonated Approach
nodes.read_aloud: A bright green comet streaks toward the ship, shedding sparkling soda hail across the windshield. The bridge smells faintly like lime syrup, which is not a reassuring development.

choices.label: Scan the soda comet
choices.result_flavor: The sensors begin slurping up extremely sticky data.

choices.label: Target it dramatically
choices.result_flavor: Captain Stretch points at the comet with crisp shrimp-denial authority.

nodes.title: Pedro Finds the Bubbles
nodes.read_aloud: Engineering reports the comet is packed with pressurized syrup crystals. Pedro has already connected one of the readings to the coolant loop because he said it looked lonely.

choices.label: Analyze the syrup math
choices.result_flavor: The crew discovers the comet has a hollow center and several terrible possibilities.

choices.label: Reroute the pressure
choices.result_flavor: Pedro reroutes something with the confidence of a raccoon opening a circuit breaker with a spoon.

nodes.title: Toothless Prepares for War
nodes.read_aloud: Toothless arrives in full security mode and declares the comet a hostile beverage. The security crew has somehow produced anti-soda shields and a net labeled 'FOR EMERGENCIES OR PARADE CANDY.'

choices.label: Deploy the anti-soda net
choices.result_flavor: The net catches part of the comet and flings a chunk of fizz into Engineering.

choices.label: Contain it in foam
choices.result_flavor: Security launches a perfect foam sphere around the comet and, regrettably, also around the ship.

nodes.title: Captain Stretch Makes It Official
nodes.read_aloud: The analysis reveals the comet can be safely vented if the crew hits the hollow core at exactly the right angle. Captain Stretch straightens his uniform, insists this is a completely normal captain problem, and demands immediate action.

commander_moment.prompt: Captain Stretch demands a precise bridge decision and absolutely refuses to answer any shrimp-related follow-up questions.

choices.label: Scan for a clean vent
choices.result_flavor: The bridge finds a narrow safe channel and prays the syrup obeys geometry.

choices.label: Target the hollow core
choices.result_flavor: Weapons lock onto the exact center of the problem, which is always encouraging right before disaster.

nodes.title: Cola Lightning in the Reactor
nodes.read_aloud: Engineering fills with crackling brown lightning as the comet syrup hits the hot lines. Pedro is wearing safety goggles over only one eye, which somehow feels worse than wearing none.

dice_hook.purpose: Determine whether the overloaded cola surge can be read before it cooks the reactor.
dice_hook.failure_text: The readings arrive one second too late and the fizz chooses violence.
dice_hook.success_text: The crew catches the surge pattern just before the reactor commits to a beverage-themed meltdown.

choices.label: Analyze the surge pattern
choices.result_flavor: The diagnostics attempt to explain cola lightning in a tone normally reserved for tax forms.

choices.label: Reroute it anyway
choices.result_flavor: Pedro shouts 'loop it back!' which has never once improved a sentence.

nodes.title: Foam Prison Drift
nodes.read_aloud: Security successfully contains the comet and accidentally cocoons the entire Starship Shamples in expanding foam. The ship drifts safely through space like a giant packing peanut while the crew debates whether this still counts as a tactical victory.

nodes.title: Sticky but Operational
nodes.read_aloud: The crew vents the comet in a glittering arc of harmless soda mist and the ship survives. Every console is now tacky forever, but Captain Stretch declares the mission a polished example of command excellence and nobody is brave enough to challenge that.

nodes.title: The Celebratory Geyser
nodes.read_aloud: The targeted shot pops the comet perfectly and launches a galaxy-sized soda fountain across the bow. The crew survives the blast, but the ship is now internationally recognized as the inventor of an accidental parade.

nodes.title: Full Soda Meltdown
nodes.read_aloud: Pedro's final reroute converts the reactor into a carbonated thunder cannon and the ship lurches out of control. The Starship Shamples survives technically, but only because the explosion blasts it into a new postal code before the paperwork can catch up.
